title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During the trial, the man identifying himself as Harakti (also referred to as Horus or Harleckt) provides erratic testimony, claiming to be a divine healer and the god Horus while citing Egyptian deities such as Nut, Isis, and Osiris. Colonel Pickert informs the coroner that Harakti is actually a traveling medicine showman who sells cod liver oil under the guise of "sunlight." However, Ellery Quine recognizes a Uraeus emblem on the man's hand, connecting the witness to ancient Egypt.

When questioned about the dark-skinned, limping man described by witness Casper Crocker, Harakti identifies the individual as his "disciple" and "Great Priest of Horus," named Verga Krossak (or Velja Krosac). Colonel Pickert clarifies that Krossak served as Harakti's business manager and advertising agent. The Colonel notes that Krossak was last seen on Christmas Eve near Holly Bay, several miles from Wilton, around 10 o'clock, and has since disappeared; authorities have no leads regarding Krossak or the witness Crocker.

As the proceedings continue, evidence is presented regarding a decapitated body nailed to a cross. It is revealed that the iron nails used in the crucifixion were taken from the victim Andrew Van's own kitchen toolbox. Just as the coroner, Mr. Swipe, begins his address to the jury, Ellery interrupts the trial. He claims to have discovered an "ancient fact" older than Christianity that is relevant to the case, drawing the curiosity of the jury and the court.
